revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:Pages with blank templates]] <noinclude> [[Category:Blank templates]] </noinclude>" to "<includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of {{subst:PAGENAME}}]]</includeonly> <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude>"
Text replacement - "<includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of {{subst:PAGENAME}}]]</includeonly>" to "[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of {{subst:PAGENAME}}]]"
revision.text: [[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Xt/see also]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude>
[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Xt/see also]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude> [[Category:unused templates]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵[[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects X]]" to " <noinclude>[[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects X]]</noinclude>"
Bot: remove deleted category Automatic wikipedia template redirects X

revision.text:
{{navbar|Xt/see also|plain=y}}
* {{tlx|xt}} inline {{xt|positive example text}}, in green, with serif font * {{tlx|xt2}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but for blocks of text * {{tlx|bxt}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but uses {{bxt|bold instead of serif font}} * {{tlx|mxt}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but uses {{mxt|mono-spaced font}} (especially useful in source code)
  * {{tlx|!xt}} inline {{!xt|negative example text}}, in red, with serif font * {{tlx|!xt2}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but for blocks (i.e., same as {{tnull|xt2}} but red) * {{tlx|!bxt}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but uses {{!bxt|boldface}} * {{tlx|!mxt}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but uses {{!mxt|mono-spaced font}}; used for incorrect or strongly deprecated code/output/input examples and should usually be wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}} as appropriate; see also {{tlx|dc}} and its variants below * {{tlx|!xts}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but also {{!xts|strikes through the text}} * {{tlx|dcr}} inline {{dcr|strongly deprecated or deleted material}}; {{tlx|dc2}} variant has {{dc2|strikethrough}} (they both use the {{tag|del|open}} element, and do not add monospace font on their own; can be used in mainspace (articles), and where necessary wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}}); see also {{tlx|dc}} below
  * {{tlx|xtd}} inline {{xtd|deprecated (or uncertain, unavailable, lorem, etc.) example text}}, in grey * {{tlx|bxtd}} same as {{tnull|xtd}} but uses {{bxtg|boldface}} * {{tlx|mxtd}} same as {{tnull|xtd}} but uses {{mxtd|mono-spaced font}} * {{tlx|dc}} inline {{dc|deprecated or deleted material}}; (uses the {{tag|del|open}} element, and does not add monospace font on it own; can be used in mainspace (articles), and where necessary wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}}); see also {{tlx|dcr}} above
  * {{tlx|xtn}} inline {{xtn|neutral example text}}, with no color change, when none of the above applies; used for "permissible" examples neither favored nor deprecated * {{tlx|xtn2}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but for blocks of text * {{tlx|bxtn}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but uses {{bxtn|boldface}}; it still applies a CSS class, so it's not simply boldfacing * {{tlx|mxtn}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but uses {{mxtn|mono-spaced font}}; this is a good template to use when the shaded box formatting of {{tag|code}} might be undesirable, or the [[Semantic HTML|semantics]] of it is incorrect in the context{{#if:{{{sb|}}}||
 }} * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{documentation}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{navbar|Xt/see also|plain=y}}
* {{tlx|xt}} inline {{xt|positive example text}}, in green, with serif font * {{tlx|xt2}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but for blocks of text * {{tlx|bxt}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but uses {{bxt|bold instead of serif font}} * {{tlx|mxt}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but uses {{mxt|mono-spaced font}} (especially useful in source code)
  * {{tlx|!xt}} inline {{!xt|negative example text}}, in red, with serif font * {{tlx|!xt2}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but for blocks (i.e., same as {{tnull|xt2}} but red) * {{tlx|!bxt}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but uses {{!bxt|boldface}} * {{tlx|!mxt}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but uses {{!mxt|mono-spaced font}}; used for incorrect or strongly deprecated code/output/input examples and should usually be wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}} as appropriate; see also {{tlx|dc}} and its variants below * {{tlx|!xts}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but also {{!xts|strikes through the text}} * {{tlx|dcr}} inline {{dcr|strongly deprecated or deleted material}}; {{tlx|dc2}} variant has {{dc2|strikethrough}} (they both use the {{tag|del|open}} element, and do not add monospace font on their own; can be used in mainspace (articles), and where necessary wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}}); see also {{tlx|dc}} below
  * {{tlx|xtd}} inline {{xtd|deprecated (or uncertain, unavailable, lorem, etc.) example text}}, in grey * {{tlx|bxtd}} same as {{tnull|xtd}} but uses {{bxtg|boldface}} * {{tlx|mxtd}} same as {{tnull|xtd}} but uses {{mxtd|mono-spaced font}} * {{tlx|dc}} inline {{dc|deprecated or deleted material}}; (uses the {{tag|del|open}} element, and does not add monospace font on it own; can be used in mainspace (articles), and where necessary wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}}); see also {{tlx|dcr}} above
  * {{tlx|xtn}} inline {{xtn|neutral example text}}, with no color change, when none of the above applies; used for "permissible" examples neither favored nor deprecated * {{tlx|xtn2}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but for blocks of text * {{tlx|bxtn}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but uses {{bxtn|boldface}}; it still applies a CSS class, so it's not simply boldfacing * {{tlx|mxtn}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but uses {{mxtn|mono-spaced font}}; this is a good template to use when the shaded box formatting of {{tag|code}} might be undesirable, or the [[:en:Semantic HTML|semantics]] of it is incorrect in the context{{#if:{{{sb|}}}||
 }} * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{documentation}}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text:
{{navbar|Xt/see also|plain=y}}
* {{tlx|xt}} inline {{xt|positive example text}}, in green, with serif font * {{tlx|xt2}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but for blocks of text * {{tlx|bxt}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but uses {{bxt|bold instead of serif font}} * {{tlx|mxt}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but uses {{mxt|mono-spaced font}} (especially useful in source code)
  * {{tlx|!xt}} inline {{!xt|negative example text}}, in red, with serif font * {{tlx|!xt2}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but for blocks (i.e., same as {{tnull|xt2}} but red) * {{tlx|!bxt}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but uses {{!bxt|boldface}} * {{tlx|!mxt}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but uses {{!mxt|mono-spaced font}}; used for incorrect or strongly deprecated code/output/input examples and should usually be wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}} as appropriate; see also {{tlx|dc}} and its variants below * {{tlx|!xts}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but also {{!xts|strikes through the text}} * {{tlx|dcr}} inline {{dcr|strongly deprecated or deleted material}}; {{tlx|dc2}} variant has {{dc2|strikethrough}} (they both use the {{tag|del|open}} element, and do not add monospace font on their own; can be used in mainspace (articles), and where necessary wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}}); see also {{tlx|dc}} below
  * {{tlx|xtd}} inline {{xtd|deprecated (or uncertain, unavailable, lorem, etc.) example text}}, in grey * {{tlx|bxtd}} same as {{tnull|xtd}} but uses {{bxtg|boldface}} * {{tlx|mxtd}} same as {{tnull|xtd}} but uses {{mxtd|mono-spaced font}} * {{tlx|dc}} inline {{dc|deprecated or deleted material}}; (uses the {{tag|del|open}} element, and does not add monospace font on it own; can be used in mainspace (articles), and where necessary wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}}); see also {{tlx|dcr}} above
  * {{tlx|xtn}} inline {{xtn|neutral example text}}, with no color change, when none of the above applies; used for "permissible" examples neither favored nor deprecated * {{tlx|xtn2}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but for blocks of text * {{tlx|bxtn}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but uses {{bxtn|boldface}}; it still applies a CSS class, so it's not simply boldfacing * {{tlx|mxtn}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but uses {{mxtn|mono-spaced font}}; this is a good template to use when the shaded box formatting of {{tag|code}} might be undesirable, or the [[:en:Semantic HTML|semantics]] of it is incorrect in the context{{#if:{{{sb|}}}||
 }} * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{documentation}} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text:
{{navbar|Xt/see also|plain=y}}
* {{tlx|xt}} inline {{xt|positive example text}}, in green, with serif font * {{tlx|xt2}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but for blocks of text * {{tlx|bxt}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but uses {{bxt|bold instead of serif font}} * {{tlx|mxt}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but uses {{mxt|mono-spaced font}} (especially useful in source code)
  * {{tlx|!xt}} inline {{!xt|negative example text}}, in red, with serif font * {{tlx|!xt2}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but for blocks (i.e., same as {{tnull|xt2}} but red) * {{tlx|!bxt}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but uses {{!bxt|boldface}} * {{tlx|!mxt}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but uses {{!mxt|mono-spaced font}}; used for incorrect or strongly deprecated code/output/input examples and should usually be wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}} as appropriate; see also {{tlx|dc}} and its variants below * {{tlx|!xts}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but also {{!xts|strikes through the text}} * {{tlx|dcr}} inline {{dcr|strongly deprecated or deleted material}}; {{tlx|dc2}} variant has {{dc2|strikethrough}} (they both use the {{tag|del|open}} element, and do not add monospace font on their own; can be used in mainspace (articles), and where necessary wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}}); see also {{tlx|dc}} below
  * {{tlx|xtd}} inline {{xtd|deprecated (or uncertain, unavailable, lorem, etc.) example text}}, in grey * {{tlx|bxtd}} same as {{tnull|xtd}} but uses {{bxtg|boldface}} * {{tlx|mxtd}} same as {{tnull|xtd}} but uses {{mxtd|mono-spaced font}} * {{tlx|dc}} inline {{dc|deprecated or deleted material}}; (uses the {{tag|del|open}} element, and does not add monospace font on it own; can be used in mainspace (articles), and where necessary wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}}); see also {{tlx|dcr}} above
  * {{tlx|xtn}} inline {{xtn|neutral example text}}, with no color change, when none of the above applies; used for "permissible" examples neither favored nor deprecated * {{tlx|xtn2}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but for blocks of text * {{tlx|bxtn}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but uses {{bxtn|boldface}}; it still applies a CSS class, so it's not simply boldfacing * {{tlx|mxtn}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but uses {{mxtn|mono-spaced font}}; this is a good template to use when the shaded box formatting of {{tag|code}} might be undesirable, or the [[:en:Semantic HTML|semantics]] of it is incorrect in the context{{#if:{{{sb|}}}||
 }} * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{documentation}}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text:
{{navbar|Xt/see also|plain=y}}
* {{tlx|xt}} inline {{xt|positive example text}}, in green, with serif font * {{tlx|xt2}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but for blocks of text * {{tlx|bxt}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but uses {{bxt|bold instead of serif font}} * {{tlx|mxt}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but uses {{mxt|mono-spaced font}} (especially useful in source code)
  * {{tlx|!xt}} inline {{!xt|negative example text}}, in red, with serif font * {{tlx|!xt2}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but for blocks (i.e., same as {{tnull|xt2}} but red) * {{tlx|!bxt}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but uses {{!bxt|boldface}} * {{tlx|!mxt}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but uses {{!mxt|mono-spaced font}}; used for incorrect or strongly deprecated code/output/input examples and should usually be wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}} as appropriate; see also {{tlx|dc}} and its variants below * {{tlx|!xts}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but also {{!xts|strikes through the text}} * {{tlx|dcr}} inline {{dcr|strongly deprecated or deleted material}}; {{tlx|dc2}} variant has {{dc2|strikethrough}} (they both use the {{tag|del|open}} element, and do not add monospace font on their own; can be used in mainspace (articles), and where necessary wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}}); see also {{tlx|dc}} below
  * {{tlx|xtd}} inline {{xtd|deprecated (or uncertain, unavailable, lorem, etc.) example text}}, in grey * {{tlx|bxtd}} same as {{tnull|xtd}} but uses {{bxtg|boldface}} * {{tlx|mxtd}} same as {{tnull|xtd}} but uses {{mxtd|mono-spaced font}} * {{tlx|dc}} inline {{dc|deprecated or deleted material}}; (uses the {{tag|del|open}} element, and does not add monospace font on it own; can be used in mainspace (articles), and where necessary wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}}); see also {{tlx|dcr}} above
  * {{tlx|xtn}} inline {{xtn|neutral example text}}, with no color change, when none of the above applies; used for "permissible" examples neither favored nor deprecated * {{tlx|xtn2}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but for blocks of text * {{tlx|bxtn}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but uses {{bxtn|boldface}}; it still applies a CSS class, so it's not simply boldfacing * {{tlx|mxtn}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but uses {{mxtn|mono-spaced font}}; this is a good template to use when the shaded box formatting of {{tag|code}} might be undesirable, or the [[:en:Semantic HTML|semantics]] of it is incorrect in the context{{#if:{{{sb|}}}||
 }} * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{documentation}} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 1 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23694499169|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{navbar|Xt/see also|plain=y}}
* {{tlx|xt}} inline {{xt|positive example text}}, in green, with serif font * {{tlx|xt2}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but for blocks of text * {{tlx|bxt}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but uses {{bxt|bold instead of serif font}} * {{tlx|mxt}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but uses {{mxt|mono-spaced font}} (especially useful in source code)
  * {{tlx|!xt}} inline {{!xt|negative example text}}, in red, with serif font * {{tlx|!xt2}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but for blocks (i.e., same as {{tnull|xt2}} but red) * {{tlx|!bxt}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but uses {{!bxt|boldface}} * {{tlx|!mxt}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but uses {{!mxt|mono-spaced font}}; used for incorrect or strongly deprecated code/output/input examples and should usually be wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}} as appropriate; see also {{tlx|dc}} and its variants below * {{tlx|!xts}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but also {{!xts|strikes through the text}} * {{tlx|dcr}} inline {{dcr|strongly deprecated or deleted material}}; {{tlx|dc2}} variant has {{dc2|strikethrough}} (they both use the {{tag|del|open}} element, and do not add monospace font on their own; can be used in mainspace (articles), and where necessary wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}}); see also {{tlx|dc}} below
  * {{tlx|xtd}} inline {{xtd|deprecated (or uncertain, unavailable, lorem, etc.) example text}}, in grey * {{tlx|bxtd}} same as {{tnull|xtd}} but uses {{bxtg|boldface}} * {{tlx|mxtd}} same as {{tnull|xtd}} but uses {{mxtd|mono-spaced font}} * {{tlx|dc}} inline {{dc|deprecated or deleted material}}; (uses the {{tag|del|open}} element, and does not add monospace font on it own; can be used in mainspace (articles), and where necessary wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}}); see also {{tlx|dcr}} above
  * {{tlx|xtn}} inline {{xtn|neutral example text}}, with no color change, when none of the above applies; used for "permissible" examples neither favored nor deprecated * {{tlx|xtn2}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but for blocks of text * {{tlx|bxtn}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but uses {{bxtn|boldface}}; it still applies a CSS class, so it's not simply boldfacing * {{tlx|mxtn}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but uses {{mxtn|mono-spaced font}}; this is a good template to use when the shaded box formatting of {{tag|code}} might be undesirable, or the [[:en:Semantic HTML|semantics]] of it is incorrect in the context{{#if:{{{sb|}}}||
 }} * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{documentation}} [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

{{navbar|Xt/see also|plain=y}}
* {{tlx|xt}} inline {{xt|positive example text}}, in green, with serif font * {{tlx|xt2}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but for blocks of text * {{tlx|bxt}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but uses {{bxt|bold instead of serif font}} * {{tlx|mxt}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but uses {{mxt|mono-spaced font}} (especially useful in source code)
  * {{tlx|!xt}} inline {{!xt|negative example text}}, in red, with serif font * {{tlx|!xt2}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but for blocks (i.e., same as {{tnull|xt2}} but red) * {{tlx|!bxt}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but uses {{!bxt|boldface}} * {{tlx|!mxt}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but uses {{!mxt|mono-spaced font}}; used for incorrect or strongly deprecated code/output/input examples and should usually be wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}} as appropriate; see also {{tlx|dc}} and its variants below * {{tlx|!xts}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but also {{!xts|strikes through the text}} * {{tlx|dcr}} inline {{dcr|strongly deprecated or deleted material}}; {{tlx|dc2}} variant has {{dc2|strikethrough}} (they both use the {{tag|del|open}} element, and do not add monospace font on their own; can be used in mainspace (articles), and where necessary wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}}); see also {{tlx|dc}} below
  * {{tlx|xtd}} inline {{xtd|deprecated (or uncertain, unavailable, lorem, etc.) example text}}, in grey * {{tlx|bxtd}} same as {{tnull|xtd}} but uses {{bxtg|boldface}} * {{tlx|mxtd}} same as {{tnull|xtd}} but uses {{mxtd|mono-spaced font}} * {{tlx|dc}} inline {{dc|deprecated or deleted material}}; (uses the {{tag|del|open}} element, and does not add monospace font on it own; can be used in mainspace (articles), and where necessary wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}}); see also {{tlx|dcr}} above
  * {{tlx|xtn}} inline {{xtn|neutral example text}}, with no color change, when none of the above applies; used for "permissible" examples neither favored nor deprecated * {{tlx|xtn2}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but for blocks of text * {{tlx|bxtn}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but uses {{bxtn|boldface}}; it still applies a CSS class, so it's not simply boldfacing * {{tlx|mxtn}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but uses {{mxtn|mono-spaced font}}; this is a good template to use when the shaded box formatting of {{tag|code}} might be undesirable, or the [[:en:Semantic HTML|semantics]] of it is incorrect in the context{{#if:{{{sb|}}}||
 }} * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{documentation}} [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/25015587690|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{navbar|Xt/see also|plain=y}}
* {{tlx|xt}} inline {{xt|positive example text}}, in green, with serif font * {{tlx|xt2}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but for blocks of text * {{tlx|bxt}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but uses {{bxt|bold instead of serif font}} * {{tlx|mxt}} same as {{tnull|xt}} but uses {{mxt|mono-spaced font}} (especially useful in source code)
  * {{tlx|!xt}} inline {{!xt|negative example text}}, in red, with serif font * {{tlx|!xt2}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but for blocks (i.e., same as {{tnull|xt2}} but red) * {{tlx|!bxt}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but uses {{!bxt|boldface}} * {{tlx|!mxt}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but uses {{!mxt|mono-spaced font}}; used for incorrect or strongly deprecated code/output/input examples and should usually be wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}} as appropriate; see also {{tlx|dc}} and its variants below * {{tlx|!xts}} same as {{tnull|!xt}} but also {{!xts|strikes through the text}} * {{tlx|dcr}} inline {{dcr|strongly deprecated or deleted material}}; {{tlx|dc2}} variant has {{dc2|strikethrough}} (they both use the {{tag|del|open}} element, and do not add monospace font on their own; can be used in mainspace (articles), and where necessary wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}}); see also {{tlx|dc}} below
  * {{tlx|xtd}} inline {{xtd|deprecated (or uncertain, unavailable, lorem, etc.) example text}}, in grey * {{tlx|bxtd}} same as {{tnull|xtd}} but uses {{bxtg|boldface}} * {{tlx|mxtd}} same as {{tnull|xtd}} but uses {{mxtd|mono-spaced font}} * {{tlx|dc}} inline {{dc|deprecated or deleted material}}; (uses the {{tag|del|open}} element, and does not add monospace font on it own; can be used in mainspace (articles), and where necessary wrapped in {{tag|code|o}}, {{tag|samp|o}}, or {{tag|kbd|o}}); see also {{tlx|dcr}} above
  * {{tlx|xtn}} inline {{xtn|neutral example text}}, with no color change, when none of the above applies; used for "permissible" examples neither favored nor deprecated * {{tlx|xtn2}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but for blocks of text * {{tlx|bxtn}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but uses {{bxtn|boldface}}; it still applies a CSS class, so it's not simply boldfacing * {{tlx|mxtn}} same as {{tnull|xtn}} but uses {{mxtn|mono-spaced font}}; this is a good template to use when the shaded box formatting of {{tag|code}} might be undesirable, or the [[:en:Semantic HTML|semantics]] of it is incorrect in the context{{#if:{{{sb|}}}||
 }} * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{documentation}} [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
